data.title: Whales obtained 1% of circulating BTC in last 4 months, also branching into alternative cryptos amidst market uncertainties.
data.description: In the past few months, large Bitcoin holders have acquired close to 1% of the total circulating supply of BTC. These "whales" have been expanding their portfolios by investing in other cryptocurrencies as well, indicating a strategy of diversification. This trend comes at a time when the market shows conflicting signals, showcasing the evolving landscape of digital asset investments.

data.title: Agrace to take over hospice program at Crossing Rivers Health, Wisconsin's largest nonprofit hospice. #partnership
data.description: In Prairie du Chien, Wisconsin on July 31, 2025, Agrace, the state's leading nonprofit hospice provider, has revealed a collaboration with Crossing Rivers Health to acquire the health system's hospice initiative. This collaboration marks an essential step in their partnership, as the two entities have solidified a Preferred Partner agreement.

data.title: Trek Bicycle to use low-emission aluminum in frames starting Oct 2025, sourced from eco-friendly facilities.
data.description: In Waterloo, Wisconsin, as of July 31, 2025, it has been announced that beginning in October 2025, a significant change will take place in the production of aluminum bicycle frames. Companies such as Trek, Electra, and Diamant will transition to using aluminum sourced from facilities powered by renewable energy or incorporating recycled materials for nearly all their bicycle frames. This shift towards sustainable sourcing will have a notable impact on the environmental footprint of the industry.

data.title: Money2, the emerging financial system with $225 billion in stablecoins, is reshaping value transfer through DeFi and code-based contracts.
data.description: Money2, a recently introduced financial system driven by stablecoins and decentralized finance (DeFi), is revolutionizing the transfer of value. With a staggering $225 billion invested in stablecoins and automated contracts overtaking traditional banking systems, Money2 is reshaping the financial landscape.

data.title: PickleRage opens new franchise club in Eldersburg, Maryland, expanding its indoor pickleball presence.
data.description: An esteemed indoor pickleball club franchise, PickleRage, has joined forces with franchisees to introduce a cutting-edge indoor pickleball experience to Eldersburg, Maryland. The venture marks the opening of their inaugural franchise on July 31, 2025.

data.title: Pokeworks sees significant growth with four new openings and upgraded tech in 2025, setting pace for franchise targets.
data.description: In Irvine, California, on July 31, 2025, Pokeworks, the top premium poke brand, is experiencing significant growth with the opening of four new locations. The brand's global expansion is further fueled by securing multi-unit deals and implementing upgraded technology. As the fastest-growing fast-casual poke brand worldwide, Pokeworks is poised for a successful second half of 2025 with strong performance and continued momentum.

data.title: Like Air® partners with Prime Bites to launch Chocolate Glazed Donut Puffcorn and Protein Brownies Bundle on TikTok Shop.
data.description: A collaboration between snack company Like Air® and Prime Bites has introduced a new bundle on TikTok Shop. This partnership brings a unique combination of Chocolate Glazed Donut Puffcorn and Protein Brownies. Like Air®, known for its quality snacks, has joined hands with Prime Bites for the first time to create this exclusive product. The bundle is set to capture the attention of snack lovers, as it offers a delicious mix of sweet and savory flavors. The release date for this bundle is July 31, 2025, and it marks a significant milestone for both companies as they venture into this joint venture. This innovative collaboration is expected to cater to a wide audience, given the popularity of both brands. It is a move that showcases the commitment of Like Air® and Prime Bites to bring unique and enticing products to the market.

data.title: Foresight Technology VCT PLC reports 2025 results: £38.4m net assets, 9.8m new funds raised, 3.0m post-reporting funds boosted, 7 new investments made, 3 follow-ons, total deployment £33.2m. Management fee £721,000, responsible investing focus, upcoming AGM. Buyback policy in effect, UK VCT scheme extended, Chairman notes challenging economic conditions, focus on tech investments, £42.2m raised, £30.0m investments in 36 companies, positive fundraising momentum, strong investment pipeline, audited financials show a loss, Investment Manager optimistic on portfolio returns.
data.description: Foresight Technology VCT plc announced its final results for the year ended March 31, 2025, with total Net Assets at £38.4m and Net Asset Value per share at 91.4p. During the year, they raised £9.8m under subscription offers. Investments were made in six new and eleven follow-on companies totaling £9.8m. The company also completed the purchase of 88,986 of its own shares. Since then, an additional £3.0m was raised, bringing total funds raised to £45.2m. The company had investments totaling £30m in 36 companies. They made one new investment and three follow-on investments post the reporting period totalling £3.2m. The company increased total funds raised to over £45.2m. The annual management fee was £721,000. The Investment Manager focused on driving investments for realization and managing a difficult operating environment for early-stage businesses. The board noted the UK VCT scheme's extension by ten years to April 2035, providing support for further investments in early-stage companies. The global economic conditions were challenging, with spotlight on UK-based high technology companies. The government's Science and Technology Framework aimed to unlock over £20 billion for research & development, creating potential opportunities for technology companies. The Investment Manager continued to see a strong pipeline of new opportunities. They invested in new companies like Cavero Quantum Limited, Xim Limited, Bitfount Limited, and others, totaling £9.8m during the year. The company also made investments post the reporting period, seeing a total deployment of £33.2m. Further fundraising efforts post the year-end raised an additional £3.0m, making the total funds raised over £45.2m. The Investment Manager detailed portfolio movements, with net investment holding losses totaling £2.2m during the year. The audited financial statements showed a loss after taxation of £3.07m for the year ended March 31, 2025. The article also highlights significant details from the Investment Manager's review, audited financial statements, and the company's outlook in the uncertain global economic and investment landscape.

data.title: Inner Haven Wellness expands insurance coverage with Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield in Wisconsin, allowing thousands more to access eating disorder treatment program.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, Inner Haven Wellness of Madison, Wisconsin, revealed a recent collaboration with Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield, a leading health insurance provider in the state. This new in-network insurance agreement signifies a major breakthrough in healthcare accessibility, granting thousands of individuals the opportunity to receive covered services at Inner Haven Wellness.

data.title: Specialty Program Group bought ANOVA Marine Insurance to expand transportation capabilities on July 31, 2025.
data.description: Specialty Program Group LLC (SPG), a prominent operator in specialty insurance distribution, underwriting, and consulting, recently announced the acquisition of ANOVA Marine Insurance Services, LLC ("ANOVA"). The acquisition, finalized on July 31, 2025, represents SPG's strategic move towards expanding its services portfolio. ANOVA, known for its advanced technology and comprehensive marine insurance offerings, is expected to bolster SPG's market presence and enhance its capabilities in the marine insurance sector. This acquisition marks another milestone for SPG as they continue to strengthen their position in the industry.

data.title: New book by New York rabbi explores faith in modern world targeting multi-denominational audience.
data.description: In New York on July 31, 2025, a new book titled "A Truth versus The Truth: How Religious Individuals Navigate the Modern World" is set to be released by Rabbi Stephen B. Roberts. This publication intends to cater to a diverse audience across various religious denominations. The book explores the intersection of faith and the contemporary society, offering insights into how religious individuals either embrace or resist aspects of the modern world. It provides a comprehensive analysis of this dynamic, appealing to a wide range of readers interested in understanding the complexities of faith in today's society.
